name: SMM (Standard Mineral Medium, ref [18]) + 10 mM glycerol (PMC6921056)
description: SMM (Standard Mineral Medium, ref [18]) + 10 mM glycerol — medium reported in PMC6921056 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC6921056 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Full recipe recovered from the primary literature: base medium 'SMM (Synthetic Mineral Medium)' (Cited in the paper as ref [18]: Kurumbang N.P., Dvorak P., Bendl J., Brezovsky J., Prokop Z., Damborsky J. Computer-assisted engineering of the synthetic pathway for biodegradation of a toxic persistent pollutant. ACS Synth. Biol. 2014;3:172-181. DOI 10.1021/sb400147n; PMID 24313542. The full SMM composition (identical medium, same E. coli BL21(DE3) TCP-degradation system, same co-authors) is given v
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.verification_note: PMC6921056 does NOT list the chemical composition of SMM (Synthetic/Standard Mineral Medium); the entire mineral base (salts, phosphate buffer, ammonium N-source, trace elements) is deferred to external reference [18] (Dvořák et al.), which I could not access. Therefore the full formulation cannot be verified or reconstructed from this paper without fabrication -> left for manual review (pull comp
provenance.recipe_evidence: From P. Dvořák PhD dissertation (same lab/strain/system as ref [18]): "A synthetic mineral medium (SMM) containing 5.4 g of Na2HPO4.12H2O, 1.4 g of KH2PO4, 0.5 g of (NH4)2SO4, 0.2 g of MgSO4.7H2O, 2 ml of trace elements solution and 1 ml of vitamin B1 (10 g.ml-1) per 1 l was used for selection experiments [274]." Target paper PMC6921056 Methods: "...resuspended in Synthetic Mineral Medium (SMM) [18]. SMM medium (15 mL) with 10 mM glycerol in 25-mL Reacti Flask closed with screw cap was preincubated at 37 C, growth test was initiated by addition of cells to 0.1 OD600."
provenance.base_medium: SMM (Synthetic Mineral Medium), a defined phosphate-buffered E. coli mineral medium (Na2HPO4/KH2PO4 + (NH4)2SO4 + MgSO4 + trace elements + thiamine), from Kurumbang et al. 2014 (ref 18), originally attributed to Assis et al. 1998 (ref 274).
provenance.base_medium_reference: Cited in the paper as ref [18]: Kurumbang N.P., Dvorak P., Bendl J., Brezovsky J., Prokop Z., Damborsky J. Computer-assisted engineering of the synthetic pathway for biodegradation of a toxic persistent pollutant. ACS Synth. Biol. 2014;3:172-181. DOI 10.1021/sb400147n; PMID 24313542. The full SMM composition (identical medium, same E. coli BL21(DE3) TCP-degradation system, same co-authors) is given verbatim in the PhD dissertation of P. Dvořák, Masaryk University (https://is.muni.cz/th/151419/prif_d/Dvorak_dizertace.pdf), which cites Assis H.M.S., Sallis P.J., Bull A.T., Hardman D.J. Biochemical characterization of a haloalcohol dehalogenase from Arthrobacter erithii H10a. Enzyme Microb. Technol. 1998;22:568-574 as the origin of the mineral base + trace-element/vitamin solutions.
provenance.verification_evidence: SMM medium (15 mL) with 10 mM glycerol in 25-mL Reacti Flask closed with screw cap was preincubated at 37 C, growth test was initiated by addition of cells to 0.1 OD600.

sources_chased.what: Target paper PMC6921056 main text (defines only 'SMM [18] + 10 mM glycerol', no composition)
Sibling open-access paper Dvorak et al. 2015 Microb. Cell Fact. (PMC4687329, same group/strain/pathway) to test for SMM recipe
added: negative result: that paper uses LB + phosphate buffer, NOT SMM; recipe not there

sources_chased.what: Base recipe cited as ref [18] (Kurumbang et al. 2014, ACS Synth. Biol., DOI 10.1021/sb400147n) - paywalled, not in PMC
sources_chased.how: Recovered verbatim via the compilation PhD dissertation of P. Dvorak (defended 2014, MU Brno) which reproduces the Kurumbang 2014 Materials & Methods; located through theses.cz/id/inya2d and downloaded is.muni.cz/th/gyoy9/Dvorak_dizertace.pdf (cookie-handshake)

sources_chased.what: Trace-elements & vitamin stock composition, cited as ref [274] Assis et al. 1998 (Enzyme Microb. Technol. 22:568-574)
sources_chased.how: Elsevier paywalled; Kent Academic Repository (kar.kent.ac.uk/17553) holds metadata only, no open full text
added: trace elements solution LEFT UNEXPANDED (constituent metal salts not invented)

recuration.notes: Full SMM bulk-salt recipe recovered from the Kurumbang 2014 (ref 18) Materials & Methods, obtained verbatim from P. Dvorak's compilation PhD dissertation. The four bulk salts, thiamine and glycerol are fully quantified; the 2 mL/L trace-elements solution is left unexpanded because its constituent salts live only in the paywalled Assis 1998 (ref 274) and were not recoverable. Current record was missing Na2HPO4 (the sodium/main phosphate-buffer component) and the trace-elements solution; both are now confirmed real.
